definition: Support for the concerns of ordinary people
example: Populism gives voice to the forgotten man.

definition: Put to death by nailing or binding the hands and feet to a cross
example: You shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.

definition: A circular ornamental headdress worn by a monarch
example: Burn down your cities and leave our farms, and your cities will spring up again as if by magic; but destroy our farms and the grass will grow in the streets of every city in the country.

definition: The chair on which a sovereign is seated on state occasions
example: Having behind us the producing masses of this nation, we shall answer their demands for a gold standard by saying to them: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns.

definition: A stiff, sharp-pointed woody projection on a stem or plant
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns.

definition: A system or theory using only one metal as standard currency
example: Monometallism serves the interests of the wealthy few.

definition: The use of two metals, gold and silver, as currency
example: Bimetallism will bring prosperity to the farmer and laborer.

definition: Having luster; shining; bright; radiant
example: The name of the Lord is lustrous and bright.

definition: A garden near Jerusalem where Jesus prayed before his crucifixion
example: From the garden of Gethsemane to the mount of Calvary, the savior walked.

definition: The hill near Jerusalem where Jesus was crucified
example: From Gethsemane to Calvary, the path of sacrifice.

definition: A fence or boundary formed by closely growing bushes or shrubs
example: Our farms are the hedge that protects American prosperity.

definition: Producing or capable of producing abundant vegetation or crops
example: The fertile prairies of the Midwest feed the nation.

definition: A large area of flat land with few trees
example: The vast plains of our country shall not be dominated by eastern financiers.

definition: A low area of land between hills or mountains
example: From the valleys to the mountaintops, we demand justice.

definition: People who work for wages
example: The wage-earners of this country deserve fair treatment.

definition: Directing one's hopes or ambitions toward becoming a specified type of person
example: The aspiring masses seek only a fair chance.

definition: No longer remembered
example: We must not let the forgotten man be cast aside.

definition: A heavy iron block on which hot metal is hammered into shape
example: Upon this anvil of public opinion, we shall forge a new currency.

definition: A tool with a heavy metal head mounted at right angles at the end of a handle
example: The hammer of the people shall ring upon the anvil of justice.

definition: Clothing; garments; dress
example: The raiment of the wealthy is woven from the labor of the poor.
